steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: By linking the Hudson River to Lake Erie is an introductory participial phrase. The main clause begins with the canal reduced transport time, so the introductory phrase needs only a comma before the subject.

steps.title: Use the comma
steps.content: A semicolon and D colon would require an independent clause before the punctuation, and B incorrectly opens a parenthesis before the main clause. A comma correctly separates the introductory phrase from the sentence's main clause. The answer is C.
